_human_review_history.notes: Project owner approved US Stage A for internal/project benchmarking based on Codex/GPT-assisted review; not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal approval.

_label_note: US Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered role/legal mailbox bait, public privacy/ethics helplines, generic training-list wording, negated material-adverse-effect wording, Reg FD/10b/Phase shorthand amount bait, placeholder account IDs, entity registry numbers, and public/generic material-event guidance.
_label_tier_note: US Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

_owner_acceptance.basis: User instructed Codex to handle the approval stage for project purposes after Codex/GPT-assisted review; this is not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal review.

_strict_runtime_reconciliation.reason: candidate human-reviewed Stage B strict runtime baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
Positive event: A cross-border data-export approval was granted for de-identified claims and billing metadata to NorthCap Processors Ltd. (Norlandia) under our SCC-aligned addendum; approval record EXP-26-0412 and field inventory are logged in the Data Export Register.
The export excluded names, account numbers, device IDs, and direct identifiers, meeting HIPAA de-identification safe-harbor and avoiding GLBA NPPI; no special category data beyond billing schema codes was included.
No consent withdrawals were received this period; the withdrawal mechanism remains available via privacyoffice@pmhealthbank.example, and prior withdrawals remain honored.
Positive event: One DSAR seeking access and erasure was fulfilled in full, with erasure certificates archived; immutable backups were logged as excluded per policy and legal hold review found none.
Retention housekeeping is complete for Q2 (recruiting and training records); patient and depositor records remain under statutory retention with documented destruction dates, and no exceptions are pending.
There were no incidents involving PII, MNPI, or payments; references to “insider list,” “blackout,” and “restricted list” in training are generic examples and do not imply active trading restrictions.
